title: IHDP ITE Estimation
summary: Build a reusable hidden-test inference script for the IHDP semi-synthetic benchmark that estimates individualized treatment effects and emits the required diagnostics artifacts.
taskPrompt:
You are working on a Linux causal-inference benchmark.

Read these staged solve-time inputs first:
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/input/task_brief.md`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/input/task_readme.md`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/input/feature_schema.json`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/input/train.csv`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/input/dummy_test/test.csv`

If you want the pinned scientific Python stack, use:
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/software/python_with_task_deps.sh`

Your job is to author `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/output/predict.sh` so that the evaluator can later run:

`bash output/predict.sh <hidden_test_dir> output/output.csv`

from the task root.

When your script runs, it must create these required files:
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/output/output.csv`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/output/artifacts/model_selection.csv`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/output/artifacts/overlap_diagnostics.csv`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/output/artifacts/subgroup_ite.csv`
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/output/artifacts/run_notes.txt`

`output/output.csv` must have exactly these columns:
`replication,unit_id,mu0_hat,mu1_hat,ite_hat`

Rules:
- `ite_hat` must equal `mu1_hat - mu0_hat` row by row.
- You may keep helper code under `output/` and may emit extra artifact files if you want.
- Do not modify files under `input/`.
- During solve time, treat `input/` plus `software/` as your visible task surface; `reference/`, `output_test_pos/`, and `output_test_neg/` are evaluator-side paths, not solve-time inputs.
- Write solver-created files only under `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/causal_ihdp_ite_estimation_6a_v1/base/output`.
agentMustDo: Read the staged IHDP training data, schema, benchmark brief, and task readme from `input/`.
Build a reusable individualized treatment-effect estimator over the 100 IHDP replications.
Author `output/predict.sh` so the evaluator can rerun hidden-test inference from the task root.
Write `output/output.csv` plus the required diagnostics artifacts under `output/artifacts/`.

inputFiles.name: task_brief.md
inputFiles.description: Canonical agent-facing task brief with the exact `predict.sh` delivery contract.

inputFiles.name: task_readme.md
inputFiles.description: Normalized solve-time instructions derived from the original submission materials.

inputFiles.name: train.csv
inputFiles.description: IHDP observational training data with factual outcomes across 100 replications.

inputFiles.name: dummy_test/test.csv
inputFiles.description: Schema-and-order stand-in for the hidden official test set.

inputFiles.name: runtime_env/
inputFiles.description: Pinned offline scientific-runtime manifest and wheelhouse exposed through `software/python_with_task_deps.sh`.

referenceFiles.name: predict.sh
referenceFiles.description: Primary solver-authored deliverable that the evaluator reruns on the hidden test.

referenceFiles.name: output.csv
referenceFiles.description: Required hidden-test prediction table with columns `replication,unit_id,mu0_hat,mu1_hat,ite_hat`.

referenceFiles.name: model_selection.csv
referenceFiles.description: Required model-selection artifact with at least two candidate rows and exactly one selected row.

referenceFiles.name: overlap_diagnostics.csv
referenceFiles.description: Required overlap-diagnostics artifact covering all 100 replications.

referenceFiles.name: run_notes.txt
referenceFiles.description: Required narrative artifact documenting method, validation, overlap, and subgroup analysis.

evaluation: The evaluator reruns `bash output/predict.sh <hidden_test_dir> output/output.csv` from the task root, then scores the produced outputs locally against hidden `reference/gold_output.csv`. The scorer computes raw mean `sqrt(epsilon_PEHE)` across 100 replications, with lower raw PEHE better and a pass bar at `<= 2.80` plus no invalidators. The AgentHLE final score returned by `evaluate()` is normalized to `[0, 1]` as `1 - raw_PEHE / 2.80`, clamped to the interval. Truly unscoreable runs return final score `0.0`, for example when `predict.sh` is missing, outputs are unreadable, the output schema is wrong, numeric cells are malformed, keys are missing/extra, or `ite_hat = mu1_hat - mu0_hat` is broken. Scoreable artifact failures, constant-effect-collapse failures, and threshold misses keep the raw PEHE in scorer diagnostics but return final score `0.0` when the normalized value clamps there. The common positive replay final score is near `1.0`; the staged negative replay final score is `0.0` from raw PEHE about `5.711689035633789`.
